rag_rules: Focus repairs on unresolved identifiers and type errors reported by the AGREE validator.
Prioritize fixing unresolved references to AADL features before attempting more complex repairs.
generic_rules: Repairs should only modify existing AADL features or introduce new helper declarations within the AGREE annex.
Avoid introducing new AADL elements as part of a repair; focus on correcting existing expressions.
Ensure that all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are defined within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `int`, `real`, and `bool` for scalar type declarations in AGREE.
Ensure expressions have a valid type (bool for comparisons, int/real for arithmetic).
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to add architecture to resolve AGREE validation errors.
Avoid modifying unrelated parts of the AADL model during repair.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL scope rules for component type vs. implementation annexes
